level_signals: 3+ years of experience managing high-performing teams
Lead a team of four Talent Operations professionals

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of experience managing high-performing teams.
Strong headcount planning, workforce planning, and reporting experience.
Demonstrated experience building scalable processes in high-growth environments.
Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple high-impact initiatives simultaneously.
Experience supporting global hiring and multi-jurisdiction compliance.
Data-driven decision maker with strong analytical capabilities.
Exceptional cross-functional communication and stakeholder management skills.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years in talent/recruiting operations a plus.
Experience supporting a large, complex recruiting organization (30+ recruiters preferred).

scope.team_size_estimate: 4 direct reports (Talent Operations professionals); supports ~40-person recruiting team
scope.surface: Recruiting operations infrastructure — ATS, scheduling, headcount governance, tech stack, interviewer enablement, compliance, candidate experience
scope.business_model: B2B fintech / SaaS — corporate spend management platform
core_responsibilities: Lead, coach, and develop a team of four Talent Operations professionals
Own the full recruiting tech stack (ATS, scheduling tools, sourcing tools, CRM, interview tools, etc.)
Own execution and operational tracking of the company's headcount plan in partnership with finance
Own end-to-end interview operations, including scheduling infrastructure
Own and scale core recruiting programs, including interviewer enablement, referrals, and candidate experience
Ensure recruiting processes meet compliance requirements across jurisdictions
Partner with People Ops & Legal to support international hiring expansion

green_flags: Clear team size and organizational context (4 direct reports, ~40-person recruiting org)
Well-scoped role with explicit ownership areas across tech stack, headcount, and programs
Generous benefits package including 100% medical/dental/vision, parental leave, and fertility support
Relocation support to NYC or SF offered
Company at meaningful scale ($100B+ annualized spend, 50,000+ customers)
red_flags: Location and remote eligibility not explicitly stated in the JD body — hybrid vs. remote vs. on-site is unclear
Talent/recruiting operations experience listed as 'a plus' despite being the primary function of the role — may signal misalignment in requirements framing

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (not explicitly stated in JD body; cities mentioned in relocation support context only)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed)
seniority.level (inferred from title and team leadership signals)
ambiguities: Location type is unclear — JD body mentions relocation support to 'NYC or SF' but does not specify if the role is remote, hybrid, or on-site. Cities listed are inferred from relocation context.

Talent/recruiting operations experience (3+ years) is listed as 'a plus' in a role that is entirely talent operations — treated as soft/nice-to-have per JD language but may function as a de facto hard requirement.
Whether equity is part of the compensation package is not stated.
